Title: Direct conversion of carlactonoic acid to orobanchol by cytochrome P450 CYP722C in strigolactone biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: downstream, terminal/canonical SL-forming step with BC-ring closure
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Directly converts carlactonoic acid to orobanchol through oxidation at C-18 and BC-ring closure without passing through 4-deoxyorobanchol
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for canonical orobanchol formation; established as the novel orobanchol synthase
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Rhizosphere signaling for arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi and germination stimulant for root parasitic weeds; part of strigolactone biology
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Up-regulated in roots under phosphate-deficient conditions
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated in roots under phosphate-deficient conditions
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Arabidopsis NADPH-CYP reductase (used in reconstituted enzyme assay)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in insect cells; in vitro enzyme assay with LC-MS/MS metabolite identification; qRT-PCR coexpression under phosphate deficiency
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes direct biosynthesis of orobanchol from CLA; recombinant enzyme produces orobanchol, while 4-deoxyorobanchol is not an intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of cowpea roots, DDBJ DRA008222
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VuCYP722C was identified from a cowpea root RNA-seq coexpression module containing strigolactone biosynthetic genes. 2. Its transcript was induced by phosphate deficiency together with VuD27, VuCCD7, VuCCD8, and VuMAX1. 3. Recombinant VuCYP722C converted CLA directly to orobanchol and 2'-epi-orobanchol, with a CLA+16 intermediate consistent with 18-hydroxy-CLA. 4. VuCYP722C did not convert 4-deoxyorobanchol to orobanchol, supporting a direct CLA-to-orobanchol route.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A target to modulate canonical strigolactone composition and reduce parasitic weed stimulation while potentially retaining other SL functions; useful for pathway engineering toward low-orobanchol crops
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: NA in cowpea; no in planta mutant tested in cowpea in this study
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The enzyme likely catalyzes sequential oxidation to 18-hydroxy-CLA/18-oxo-CLA followed by non-stereoselective BC-ring closure; ent-2'-epi-orobanchol was formed in vitro but not detected naturally, implying additional stereochemical control in planta.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: downstream, terminal/canonical SL-forming step with BC-ring closure
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: orobanchol; 2'-epi-orobanchol; downstream loss also abolishes solanacol accumulation in exudates
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Directly converts carlactonoic acid to orobanchol through BC-ring closure without passing through 4-deoxyorobanchol
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for canonical orobanchol formation; novel orobanchol synthase
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Rhizosphere signaling for arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi and stimulant of root parasitic weed seed germination
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Up-regulated in roots under phosphate-deficient conditions
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated in roots under phosphate-deficient conditions
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Arabidopsis NADPH-CYP reductase (used in reconstituted enzyme assay)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in insect cells with in vitro enzyme assay; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout in tomato; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling of root exudates and roots; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of SlCYP722C abolishes orobanchol and solanacol in root exudates and causes CLA accumulation, but plant architecture remains similar to wild type
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: U6 promoter for CRISPR gRNA expression in knockout construct
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlCYP722C expression increased in phosphate-deficient tomato roots together with known SL biosynthetic genes. 2. Recombinant SlCYP722C catalyzed the same CLA-to-orobanchol reaction as VuCYP722C. 3. CRISPR-Cas9 knockout lines lacked orobanchol and solanacol in root exudates and accumulated CLA in exudates and roots. 4. Upstream SL biosynthetic gene expression and plant architecture were largely unchanged, indicating a specific defect in canonical orobanchol synthesis. 5. Root exudates from knockout plants showed reduced stimulation of parasitic weed germination.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for genome editing to reduce parasitic weed germination in tomato and potentially other crops without major architectural or yield penalties; may preserve AMF signaling via released CLA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No prominent shoot branching or stem-length defect observed; no serious yield loss noted in edited plants, but broader field performance and metabolite-network effects remain to be evaluated
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: KO plants released CLA and retained near-normal architecture, suggesting canonical SLs such as orobanchol/solanacol are more important for rhizosphere signaling than for the hormonal control of shoot branching in this context.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: upstream common strigolactone pathway before canonical orobanchol branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts carlactone to carlactonoic acid but does not convert CLA to 4-deoxyorobanchol or orobanchol
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Rhizosphere signaling and parasitic weed germination stimulant via downstream canonical SLs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in roots and induced under phosphate deficiency
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in roots and induced under phosphate deficiency
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Arabidopsis NADPH-CYP reductase (used in reconstituted enzyme assay)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; heterologous expression in insect cells; in vitro enzyme assay with LC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies CLA precursor for downstream orobanchol formation but is not sufficient for CLA-to-orobanchol conversion in cowpea
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of cowpea roots, DDBJ DRA008222
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among two cowpea MAX1 homologs, Vigun09g224400 was the strongly expressed transcript selected as VuMAX1. 2. Recombinant VuMAX1 converted CL to CLA. 3. VuMAX1 did not convert CLA to 4-deoxyorobanchol or orobanchol. 4. This established that another CYP, not CYP711A/MAX1, is required for direct CLA-to-orobanchol conversion in cowpea.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as an upstream precursor-supply gene in SL pathway engineering, but alone cannot drive orobanchol formation; should be paired with downstream branch enzymes such as CYP722C
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core experimental pathway gene because its function was directly tested to exclude MAX1 as the CLA-to-orobanchol catalyst in cowpea.
